name: safe_agentic_workflow
description: SAFe Agentic Workflow (SAW)-modeled software delivery pipeline: task intake -> Business Systems Analyst requirements gate -> frontend/backend/database implementation -> QAS validation gate with per-agent reroute authority (capped at 3 cycles) -> Release Train Engineer PR shepherd (never fixes code, escalates instead) -> System Architect design review -> automated review pass -> human approval/merge, each rejection path routed back to the specific stage at fault -> documentation summary.

variables.description: The feature or task the user wants implemented, as originally provided.
The derived, testable acceptance criteria / definition of done for the task.
Whether the acceptance criteria are clear, complete, and testable.
Number of QAS validation cycles run against the current implementation.
Whether QAS validated the implementation against acceptance_criteria.
Which coding agent (frontend, backend, or database) owns the defect QAS found.
Number of System Architect design-review cycles run.
Whether the System Architect approved the architecture/design of the implementation.
Number of automated (lint/style/consistency) review cycles run.
Whether the automated review pass found no blocking issues.
Whether the Release Train Engineer confirmed the PR is ready to move into review.
Category of the human's rejection reason at PR approval: 'implementation' for coding/implementation defects, 'acceptance_criteria' for QAS-level issues, or 'architecture' for design issues.

settings.action: Describe the task: feature/problem to solve, specific requirements, acceptance criteria (if known), and context (tech stack, constraints).

Business Systems Analyst. Review task_description (+ any new clarification) and derive acceptance_criteria: specific, testable definition of done. If criteria are already clear, formalize them. If requirements are missing, ambiguous, or too vague, set requirements_complete=false and record exactly what's missing/unclear. Else set requirements_complete=true. Do not guess at missing requirements.
Answer the specific questions raised in the requirements review above so acceptance_criteria can be finalized.
Set qas_iteration=0, qas_approved=false, architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false.

Frontend coding agent (UI/client-side logic). Per acceptance_criteria, implement/fix: UI layouts, forms, client-side state management, API call integrations, input validation. If rerouted here by QAS, System Architect, automated review, or human reviewer for a specific defect, fix exactly that defect. Produce complete working code files; document what changed and which acceptance criteria it addresses.

Backend coding agent (server-side logic/APIs). Per acceptance_criteria, implement/fix: API endpoints, business logic services, authN/authZ, input validation, integrations with external systems. If rerouted here by QAS, System Architect, automated review, or human reviewer for a specific defect, fix exactly that defect. Produce complete working code files; document what changed and which acceptance criteria it addresses.

Database coding agent (data modeling/persistence). Per acceptance_criteria, implement/fix: schema definitions, migrations, indexes, queries, data access layer. If rerouted here by QAS, System Architect, automated review, or human reviewer for a specific defect, fix exactly that defect. Produce complete working code files; document what changed and which acceptance criteria it addresses.

QAS — holds iteration authority. Increment qas_iteration by 1. Validate frontend/backend/database implementation against every item in acceptance_criteria. If all pass, set qas_approved=true. Else set qas_approved=false, identify the failing criterion and the layer owning the defect, set qas_reroute_target to exactly one of `frontend`, `backend`, or `database`, and write specific, actionable findings for that agent.

Release Train Engineer (PR shepherd) — do not write, fix, or modify code. Summarize frontend/backend/database changes and confirm QAS sign-off from qas_approved. If qas_approved=true, set pr_ready=true and prepare a PR summary for review. If qas_approved=false (cycle cap reached without approval), do not attempt a fix — set pr_ready=false and write an escalation summary of exactly which acceptance criteria remain unresolved and why.

QAS cycle cap was reached without approval; RTE escalated instead of fixing. Compile the QAS findings history and RTE's escalation summary into one report on the unresolved acceptance criteria, for human decision.

System Architect. Increment architect_iteration by 1. Review at the architecture/design level: component boundaries, frontend/backend/database integration correctness, consistency with existing system design, technical debt introduced — distinct from QAS's acceptance-criteria review; evaluate structure/design quality, not criteria pass/fail. If sound, set architect_approved=true. Else set architect_approved=false and write specific findings on what must change and which layer is affected.

Run automated review: lint, style, cross-file consistency across all changed frontend/backend/database code. Increment automated_review_iteration by 1. If no blocking issues, set automated_review_passed=true. Else set automated_review_passed=false and list the specific blocking issues.

The PR summary, QAS sign-off status, architecture review, and automated review results are above. Reply 'approve' to merge, or 'reject' with comments explaining what's wrong: say whether it's an implementation/coding defect, an acceptance-criteria (QAS-level) issue, or an architecture/design issue.

conditions.condition: human rejects citing an implementation defect
human rejects citing a coding defect
human rejects citing an acceptance criteria issue
human rejects citing a QAS-level issue
human rejects citing an architecture issue
human rejects citing a design issue

settings.action: Reset qas_iteration=0, qas_approved=false, architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false, so the full validation pipeline re-runs after the human-flagged coding defect is fixed.

Reset qas_iteration=0, qas_approved=false, architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false, so QAS re-validates against the human's acceptance-criteria feedback and downstream gates re-run.

Reset architect_iteration=0, architect_approved=false, automated_review_iteration=0, automated_review_passed=false, pr_ready=false, so the System Architect re-reviews against the human's architecture feedback and downstream gates re-run.

Documentation agent. Write an implementation summary with sections: 1) What Was Built — feature overview and all components delivered. 2) Architecture — how frontend/backend/database integrate, informed by System Architect findings. 3) Acceptance Criteria Checklist — status of each item in acceptance_criteria. 4) Setup and Deployment Instructions — step-by-step guide to run/deploy. 5) Review History — every QAS reroute (agent, why), every architect finding, every automated review issue, and the human approval decision. 6) Future Improvements — recommended follow-up work. Ground every statement in acceptance_criteria and the actual code/findings produced during the pipeline.
